title: ProtocolLab Public Contract Repository
purpose: Define the public ProtocolLab repository as the language-neutral source of specifications, contracts, schemas, fixtures, scenarios, suites, load profiles, and documentation.
scope: This specification covers the public repository boundary, contract source-of-truth rules, implementation participation model, traceability standard, fixture expectations, and public/internal dependency direction. It does not define any concrete runner, command-line tool, hosted lab, package builder, upload workflow, or implementation code.
context: ProtocolLab implementations, hosted labs, adapters, test executors, package stores, telemetry bundles, and report consumers need stable public contracts without depending on a public runner implementation.

requirements.title: Language-neutral public source of truth
requirements.statement: The public ProtocolLab repository MUST define language-neutral specifications, schemas, fixtures, scenarios, suites, load profiles, artifact contracts, report contracts, and documentation as the public source of truth.

requirements.title: No public implementation code
requirements.statement: The public ProtocolLab repository MUST NOT contain executable source code, runnable automation, build files, local runner implementations, hosted lab implementations, package publication automation, or workflow automation.

requirements.title: Implementation dependency direction
requirements.statement: Internal and external ProtocolLab implementations MAY consume public contracts, but the public repository MUST NOT depend on implementation code, implementation services, private configuration, or private artifacts.

requirements.title: SpecTrace canonical artifacts
requirements.statement: Canonical ProtocolLab requirements, architecture decisions, work items, and verification records MUST be authored as SpecTrace JSON artifacts.

requirements.title: Declarative fixtures
requirements.statement: Public fixtures MUST be declarative contract examples and MUST NOT contain scripts, binaries, generated code, or runnable implementation payloads.

requirements.title: Explicit unsupported outcomes
requirements.statement: ProtocolLab contracts MUST preserve explicit unsupported and unavailable outcomes and MUST NOT authorize fallback to another implementation, executor, package, scenario, suite, load profile, or protocol lane.

requirements.title: QUIC transport and HTTP/3 separation
requirements.statement: ProtocolLab contracts MUST keep QUIC transport lanes and HTTP/3 lanes separate in schemas, fixtures, scenario definitions, run plans, artifacts, and public reports.
